source_pr.title: types: fix type merge about bit type
source_pr.body:
Signed-off-by: lzmhhh123 <lzmhhh123@gmail.com><!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #23832 

### What is changed and how it works?

How it Works: make type merge infer for bit column compatible with MySQL 8.0

### Related changes

- Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test
- Integration test

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- fix type merge about bit type.

symptoms: tk.MustQuery("select a from t union select 10 order by a").Check(testkit.Rows("1", "2", "10")) (executor/executor_test.go)
failure_signature: tk.MustQuery("select a from t union select 10 order by a").Check(testkit.Rows("1", "2", "10")) (executor/executor_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: tk.MustQuery("select a from t union select 10 order by a").Check(testkit.Rows("1", "2", "10")) (executor/executor_test.go).
fix_pattern: tk.MustQuery("select a from t union select 10 order by a").Check(testkit.Rows("1", "2", "10")) (executor/executor_test.go)

evidence: evidence: tk.MustQuery("select a from t union select 10 order by a").Check(testkit.Rows("1", "2", "10")) (executor/executor_test.go)
